name: Thornfield
region: A grain village farther south on the same road web beyond Fenwick Crossing

sensoryProfile.sight: Fresh yellow paint on the sign, granary roofs and a church spire above bare poplars, and ledgers where one handwriting gives way to another halfway through autumn.
sensoryProfile.sound: Road wind, saddle leather, and the thin domestic quiet of a village still functioning while bad records pass through it.
sensoryProfile.smell: Cold fields, cut stubble, and indoor paper dust from handled books.
sensoryProfile.touch: Harvest-bristle roads and pages gone cleaner and deader under the new scribe's hand.
sensoryProfile.atmosphere: A still-living place whose records feel subtly captured rather than openly ruined.

establishedFacts.fact: Thornfield contributes two more supplier marks to the widening route pattern.
Its ledger hand changes in late autumn, and the newer entries lose the useful margin detail that older clerks left behind.
The bookkeeping shift predates the first sickness reports by about a month.

eventsHere.event: Sylvanas notices the handoff from one scribe to another and files it as part of the road's changing shape.
